content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** integers.

You may rearrange the elements of AAA to form a new array PPP (a permutation of AAA). For this permutation PPP, construct a sequence of prefix ranges RRR where the iii-th element is defined as

Ri=max⁡(P1,P2,…,Pi)−min⁡(P1,P2,…,Pi)R_i = \max(P_1, P_2, \dots, P_i) - \min(P_1, P_2, \dots, P_i)Ri​=max(P1​,P2​,…,Pi​)−min(P1​,P2​,…,Pi​)Your goal is to **maximize** the MEX of the sequence RRR.

The MEX of a sequence is the smallest non-negative integer that does not appear in it.

Find the number of distinct permutations PPP of AAA such that the MEX of the resulting sequence RRR is the **maximum possible**.

Since the answer can be very large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN distinct space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer: the number of permutations that achieve the maximum possible MEX for the corresponding sequence RRR, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

Explanation:
Test case 111: All permutations of A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3]:

A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3] has R=[0,1,2]R = [0, 1, 2]R=[0,1,2], giving a MEX of 333.
A=[1,3,2]A = [1, 3, 2]A=[1,3,2] has R=[0,2,2]R = [0, 2, 2]R=[0,2,2], giving a MEX of 111.
A=[2,1,3]A = [2, 1, 3]A=[2,1,3] has R=[0,1,2]R = [0, 1, 2]R=[0,1,2], giving a MEX of 333.
A=[2,3,1]A = [2, 3, 1]A=[2,3,1] has R=[0,1,2]R = [0, 1, 2]R=[0,1,2], giving a MEX of 333.
A=[3,1,2]A = [3, 1, 2]A=[3,1,2] has R=[0,2,2]R = [0, 2, 2]R=[0,2,2], giving a MEX of 111.
A=[3,2,1]A = [3, 2, 1]A=[3,2,1] has R=[0,1,2]R = [0, 1, 2]R=[0,1,2], giving a MEX of 333.

Maximum possible MEX = 333, which occurs 444 times.
Test case 222: It can be verified that the maximum achievable MEX is 222, and it occurs 222 times for A=[10,11,1]A = [10, 11, 1]A=[10,11,1] and [11,10,1][11, 10, 1][11,10,1].
Test case 333: Only one permutation exists, so the answer is 111.
